title: When tool use is the right approach vs prompt-only

body.itemA: Tool use: Claude calls external functions to get real-time data, take actions, or interact with systems. Best when the task needs information Claude doesn't have, requires side effects (write to DB, send email), or involves structured computation.
body.itemB: Prompt-only: Claude answers directly from its training knowledge and the provided context. Best when the answer is in the prompt or Claude's knowledge, no external systems need to be touched, and the task is pure text transformation or reasoning.

dimensions.name: Real-time data needed
dimensions.a: Yes — tools fetch live information (weather, DB records, API calls)
dimensions.b: No — answer is in context or training data

dimensions.name: Side effects required
dimensions.a: Yes — create records, send messages, modify files
dimensions.b: No — pure reasoning or text generation

dimensions.name: Latency
dimensions.a: Higher — each tool call adds a round trip

dimensions.name: Complexity
dimensions.a: You must implement tool execution and handle the agentic loop
dimensions.b: Simple API call with no orchestration needed
